rationale.R1.1: 'Make this stronger' may centrally target the gap statement and contribution claim — both of which are present but could be sharpened (e.g., the 'know what it knows' framing is evocative but underexplored).
rationale.R1.4: The abstract relies heavily on 'first method' and 'to our knowledge' novelty claims; strengthening those requires tightening the delta vs. nearest prior work (confidence calibration, selective prediction, probing heads).
rationale.R1.6: 'Stronger' most directly reads as rhetorical/prose improvement — punchier sentences, eliminating hedges, reducing redundancy (e.g., 'first…first…first' appears three times), and sharpening the opening hook.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I primarily strengthen the prose and rhetoric (sentence-level punch, elimination of redundancy, word choice), or the conceptual framing (sharpen the gap statement, tighten the novelty claim, and better differentiate SelfProbe from calibration and selective-prediction prior work)?
